contextNotes.body: Sunzi (孫子, fl. 6th–5th c. BCE) was a military strategist traditionally identified with Sun Wu, a general of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period. The Art of War (孫子兵法) is the earliest known Chinese treatise on military strategy, emphasizing calculation, deception, and the avoidance of direct confrontation rather than brute force.

This opening passage establishes the gravitas of military affairs: warfare determines life and death (死生之地) and the survival or destruction of the state (存亡之道), requiring the ruler's utmost deliberation. The parallel structure 死生/存亡 reflects the existential stakes Sunzi ascribes to military decisions, setting the tone for a text that treats strategy as a matter of state survival rather than glory.
